EDM_and_Bass.name: Four-on-the-Floor
EDM_and_Bass.description: Kick on 1, 2, 3, 4; Snare/Clap on 2 and 4; Open Hi-Hat on offbeats (&).

EDM_and_Bass.name: 2-Step
EDM_and_Bass.description: Kick on 1 and 3.5; Snare on 2 and 4; 16th-note ghost snare shuffle.

Rock_Metal_Punk.name: Standard Backbeat
Rock_Metal_Punk.description: Kick on 1 and 3; Snare on 2 and 4; Steady 8th-note Hi-Hats.

Rock_Metal_Punk.name: D-Beat
Rock_Metal_Punk.description: Kick on 1, 2&, 3; Snare on 2 and 4; Fast 8th-note riding on Cymbals.

Rock_Metal_Punk.name: Blast Beat
Rock_Metal_Punk.description: Rapid alternating 16th-note Kick and Snare; Unison hits on Cymbals.

Rock_Metal_Punk.name: Odd-Time Groove
Rock_Metal_Punk.description: Subdivided 2-2-3; Accents on 1, 3, 5; Shifting Snare placements.

Vintage_Machine_Presets.name: Electro Pulse
Vintage_Machine_Presets.description: Kick on 1, 1.75, 2.5; Snare on 2 and 4; 16th-note mechanical Hi-Hats.

Vintage_Machine_Presets.name: Bossa Nova
Vintage_Machine_Presets.description: Kick on 1 and 3; Rimshot pattern on 1, 1.75, 2.5, 3.25, 4.

Vintage_Machine_Presets.name: Waltz
Vintage_Machine_Presets.description: Kick on 1; Snare/Hat on 2 and 3; Emphasis on the downbeat.

Vintage_Machine_Presets.name: Trap
Vintage_Machine_Presets.description: Syncopated 808 Kick; Snare on 3 (half-time); 16th/32nd note Hi-Hat triplets.

Jazz_and_Swing_Standards.name: The Swing Groove
Jazz_and_Swing_Standards.description: Spang-a-lang: Ride cymbal on 1, 2-&, 3, 4-& (triplet feel); Hi-hat foot on 2 and 4; Feathered Kick on every beat.

Jazz_and_Swing_Standards.name: Bebop Fast
Jazz_and_Swing_Standards.description: High-speed Ride pattern; Snare 'dropping bombs' (random syncopated accents); Kick drum used for punctuation rather than a pulse.

Jazz_and_Swing_Standards.name: Jazz Waltz
Jazz_and_Swing_Standards.description: Ride on 1, 2-&, 3; Hi-hat foot on 2; Snare brushes typically used for circular texture.

Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.name: Foxtrot
Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.description: Slow-Slow-Quick-Quick: Kick on 1 and 3; Snare on 2 and 4; Hi-hat plays straight quarter notes with a slight swing.

Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.name: Rhumba
Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.description: 808/CR-78 Preset: Kick on 1, 3, 4; Woodblock/Rimshot on 1, 1-&, 3, 4; Maracas playing constant 8th notes.

Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.name: Cha-Cha-Cha
Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.description: Cowbell on every beat; Scraper (Guiro) on 4-&-1; Kick on 1 with strong snare/timbale on 2, 3, 4.

Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.name: Viennese Waltz
Ballroom_and_Social_Dance.description: Fast 3/4 pulse; Heavy accent on Beat 1 (Kick); Light brushes or hats on 2 and 3.

Blues_and_Soul.name: 12-Bar Shuffle
Blues_and_Soul.description: Triplet-based 'Gallop'; Snare on 2 and 4; Kick follows the bassline shuffle (1-&, 2-&, 3-&, 4-&).

Blues_and_Soul.name: The Motown Beat
Blues_and_Soul.description: Snare on every beat (1, 2, 3, 4); Kick on 1 and 3; Tambourine doubling the snare for high-frequency drive.

patterns.name: Hard Bop
patterns.description: Aggressive swing. The 'Spang-a-lang' ride pattern is prominent, while the snare 'comps' (responds) to the soloist.

patterns.name: Shuffle Blues
patterns.description: The classic 'Chicago Shuffle'. Every beat is subdivided into triplets with the middle note missing.

patterns.name: Country Train Beat
patterns.description: A rapid 16th-note snare roll with accents on the 'off' beats, mimicking the sound of a steam locomotive.

patterns.name: Balkan 7/8
patterns.description: Common in Bulgarian music. Grouped as 2+2+3. The long beat is at the end of the bar.

patterns.name: Take Five (Jazz 5/4)
patterns.description: Classic cool jazz. Grouped as 3+2. The ride cymbal maintains a swing feel across the five beats.

patterns.name: Math Rock 11/8
patterns.description: Highly syncopated. Often played with a 'linear' technique where no two limbs hit at the same time.

patterns.name: The Overture Gallop
patterns.description: A high-energy 2/4 rhythm used for chase scenes or finales. Heavy accent on 1, with a 'rolling' snare triplet leading into 2.

patterns.name: The Cinematic Ostinato
patterns.description: Popularised by Hans Zimmer. Steady, driving 8th or 16th note staccato hits (often Taiko or Orchestral Kicks) in 4/4 or 7/8 for 'ticking clock' tension.

patterns.name: The Military March
patterns.description: A formal 2/4 or 4/4 snare-heavy pattern with frequent 'flams' and 'drags' (grace notes) to mimic marching soldiers.

patterns.name: The Burlesque / Showtune
patterns.description: A slow, 'heavy' swing. Kick on 1 and 3; massive Snare with 'splash' cymbals on 2 and 4. Often includes a 'Bump and Grind' syncopation.

patterns.name: The Habanera
patterns.description: A dotted rhythm: 1. (long), 1-and (short), 2, 2-and. It provides a sultry, Spanish-influenced tension.

patterns.name: The Polka
patterns.description: A very fast 2/4 'Um-Pah' rhythm. Kick on 1 and 2; Snare on the 'and' of every beat.

patterns.name: The Reggae One-Drop
patterns.description: Unique because beat 1 is 'empty'. The Kick and Snare hit simultaneously only on beat 3, creating a weightless, floating feel.

patterns.name: The Afrobeat (Tony Allen Style)
patterns.description: Highly complex 4/4 where the Kick drum plays a staggered, melodic pattern while the Hi-Hat stays strictly on 8th notes.

patterns.name: The Bo Diddley Beat
patterns.description: A 3-2 clave rhythm applied to the whole drum kit: 'BUMP-bump-BUMP, BUMP-BUMP.' Iconic in 'jungle' or tribal cinematic scenes.

patterns.name: The Surf Rock Wipeout
patterns.description: Constant, frantic 16th-note single-stroke rolls on the Snare or Tom-toms, punctuated by heavy Kick drum accents.

patterns.name: The Progressive House Build-Up
patterns.description: A gradual increase in intensity using a four-on-the-floor Kick pattern, layered with rising Snare rolls and open Hi-Hats leading to a drop.

patterns.name: The Dubstep Wobble
patterns.description: A half-time Snare on beat 3 with syncopated Kick patterns and heavy use of offbeat Hi-Hats, creating a 'wobbling' effect.

patterns.name: The Trance Anthem
patterns.description: A driving four-on-the-floor Kick with rolling Snare patterns on 2 and 4, combined with fast 16th-note Hi-Hats for energy.
